element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamental mechanic introduced immediately in each scenario; player learns positioning and movement before enemy complexity increases
Players discover attack ranges and movement capabilities through initial encounters before encountering spatial puzzle-solving

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Varies by scenario (Prehistory: basic combat; Ninja: stealth mechanics; Ancient China: martial arts progression)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each scenario layers unique mechanics on top of grid combat foundation; players master base system before specialized rules
Introduces resource management and tactical timing; contrasts with unlimited-use basic skills

element_introduction_order.element: Tile-imbuing abilities (healing tiles, elemental damage zones)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Expands spatial tactics beyond simple positioning to environmental control

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Scenario-dependent (some scenarios lock techniques behind story events rather than XP)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Varies; some scenarios reward observation (learning techniques by seeing enemies use them) over grinding

element_introduction_order.element: Enemy behavior variation (random encounters vs. avoidable sprites vs. scripted battles)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players adapt to scenario-specific encounter pacing; no universal enemy encounter rule

element_introduction_order.first_appears: End of each of seven initial scenarios
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Climactic boss fights; players must synthesize all learned mechanics for scenario-specific Odio variant
Advanced content requiring mastery of all prior mechanics

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Ninth chapter after unlocking Middle Ages chapter
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Endgame revelation connecting all seven scenarios through recurring antagonist pattern

typical_relief_triggers: Scenario completion and transition to new historical era
Successful defeat of scenario-specific Odio variant
typical_tension_triggers: Introduction of new scenario gimmick (stealth detection, telepathy requirements)
Skill charging attacks by enemies (multi-turn threat buildup)
Environmental hazards on grid (tiles with properties)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Episodic tension peaks at scenario conclusion (Odio boss fight); each scenario provides isolated narrative/mechanical arc with reset between historical eras; overall progression compresses difficulty as scenarios unlock, building toward interconnected endgame

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Overworld navigation contains optional dungeons and exploration areas within each scenario; stealth scenarios reward spatial awareness of enemy placement
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Technique learning through observation (watching enemies use abilities); exploration of optional areas for item discovery

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like introduction to grid combat mechanics through initial battles in Prehistory scenario; Pogo (caveman protagonist) learns positioning basics against tribe enemies
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Gimmick complexity increases per scenario; Ancient China introduces martial arts progression and skill inheritance; Ninja scenario adds stealth detection tension; Wild West introduces environmental combat complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Scenario-specific Odio bosses require full mastery of that scenario's mechanics; Middle Ages unlocks after seven scenarios, consolidating learned systems; final chapter requires synthesis of all eight protagonist mechanics
notable_spikes: Each scenario's Odio boss encounter (thematic difficulty peak per era)
Transition to Middle Ages chapter (unlock-gated difficulty gate)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of seven initial scenarios (Prehistory, Imperial China, Edo Japan, Wild West, Present Day, Near Future, Distant Future) functions as isolated narrative and mechanical unit with unique gimmicks, progression systems, and Odio confrontation; reset between scenarios
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Prehistory: The First (Pogo narrative) vs. Twilight of Edo Japan: The Infiltrator (Oboromaru stealth scenario)

notable_level_patterns.description: Combat and exploration mechanics vary per scenario beyond grid combat foundation; some use random encounters, others avoidable sprites, others scripted battles; some feature stealth, others telepathy, others observation-based learning
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Ninja scenario emphasizes stealth avoidance and detection tension; Ancient China emphasizes martial arts technique progression and student/master lineage

notable_level_patterns.description: Each scenario climaxes with confrontation against powerful enemy whose name incorporates or is 'Odio'; pattern repeats across seven scenarios, then revealed as interconnected in final chapter
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Prehistory: Odo (dinosaur god); Imperial China: Ou Di Wan Lee; Edo Japan: Ode Iou (demon daimyo)

notable_level_patterns.description: Middle Ages chapter only unlocks after completing seven initial scenarios; final ninth chapter only unlocks after Middle Ages completion; creates narrative payoff structure requiring full playthrough
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Middle Ages acts as bridge scenario; final chapter provides meta-narrative connecting all eight protagonist stories
